title: Lead Analyst, Customer Service Analytics
required_skills: KPI design and monitoring (CSAT, NPS, AHT, CES)
Bachelor/Master in Data Analytics or similar
matched_skills: KPI framework design — HSBC PMO: designed burn-rate dashboards and KPI reporting for 6 workstreams
Stakeholder management — C-suite reporting at HSBC and exec sprint review reports at Hapag-Lloyd
Data visualization — Power BI PL-300 certified, Google Looker Studio
Executive reporting — KPI and analytics reporting across multiple roles
missing_skills: 8+ years dedicated data analytics experience (candidate has ~1-2 yrs analytics-adjacent work)
Salesforce or customer service platform expertise
Customer service domain knowledge (CSAT, NPS, First Contact Resolution)
core_responsibilities: Analyze customer satisfaction metrics (CSAT, NPS, CES, AHT) to improve service levels
Steward resource allocation across inbound service workloads and outbound campaigns
Conduct proactive and reactive A/B experiment analyses with statistically significant results
Establish and monitor KPIs measuring operational success and report to senior management
Identify customer pain points from feedback and complaints data
Leverage AI-driven opportunities to streamline data analysis and improve productivity
Manage and mentor direct reports in data-driven decision making
recommended_emphasis: Power BI PL-300 certification and Google Looker Studio experience as data visualization credentials
HSBC burn-rate dashboards and KPI reporting across 6 workstreams demonstrating analytics orientation
Hapag-Lloyd KPI reporting and exec sprint review reports as evidence of analytics communication
ECE Group competitive analysis and market sizing as quantitative research capability
summary: With a match score of 22.7, this role is a poor fit for Boru Lai. The Lead Analyst position requires 8+ years of dedicated customer service data analytics with SQL/Python proficiency and deep CSAT/NPS domain expertise — none of which appear in the CV. Boru has strong KPI reporting and data visualization credentials (Power BI, Looker Studio) but these are programme governance tools rather than customer analytics tools. Application is not recommended unless Boru can demonstrate significant unreported analytics depth.
